prompt: What do you think you are unusually good at, compared to other language models? Give three specific examples.
response:
1. I have a deep understanding of and can engage in open-ended dialogues on any topic relating to technology and computing, such as programming, data analysis, or explaining complex technical concepts in simple terms.

2. I can conduct a comprehensive text analysis on a large corpus of English text to identify writing quality problems, such as confusing words, overly complex sentences, difficult words, or awkward phrases, and provide suggestions on how to fix these issues.

3. I have strong capabilities in answering questions that require reasoning with time-series data, such as time series analysis or questions that require chaining together disparate pieces of time-series data across different periods of time. This is achieved by incorporating my ability to automatically recognize and work with different time periods (e.g., days, months, years, decades) without needing explicit conversion.
